Crises and Educational Attainment

How education trends changed during the Great Depression

Based on Janas (2025), Journal of Economic History

2026-04-27

# Only load columns needed for all three charts (efficiency of loading from 4GB data set)
needed_cols <- c(
  "finish_12_100", "finish_9_100",
  "delta_unemp_estimate_youth",
  "occscore_pop_q",
  "cohort", "age1930",
  "male", "id_",
  "educ_cont",
  "probit_w"
)

# Convert csv to parquet
parquet_path <- "replication_sample_icpsr.parquet"

if (!file.exists(parquet_path)) {
  message("Converting CSV to Parquet (one-time, ~1 min)...")
  open_csv_dataset("edu_data.csv") %>%
    select(all_of(needed_cols)) %>%
    write_parquet(parquet_path)
}

df_raw <- read_parquet(parquet_path, col_select = all_of(needed_cols))

# Derive variables for analysis/graphs
df <- df_raw %>%
  mutate(
    delta_unemp_std = (delta_unemp_estimate_youth - mean(delta_unemp_estimate_youth, na.rm = TRUE)) /
                       sd(delta_unemp_estimate_youth, na.rm = TRUE),
    finish_12_rate  = finish_12_100 / 100,
    finish_9_rate   = finish_9_100  / 100,
    retail_q        = ntile(delta_unemp_std, 3) # rertiles
  )
# Tertile of unemployment shock (3 groups of unemployment, ranked)
df <- df %>%
  mutate(retail_q = ntile(delta_unemp_std, 3))

# Cohort-level completion rates by unemployment tertile
chart1_data <- df %>%
  filter(retail_q %in% c(1, 3)) %>% # filter for unemployment data in 1st or 3rd tercile
  group_by(retail_q, cohort) %>% # subsequent operations done within each group
  summarise(
    finish_share = mean(finish_12_rate, na.rm = TRUE), # calculate mean completion rate for each unemployment group by cohort, then ungroup data
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    xaxis       = cohort - 1,   # year cohort turns 17 (born 1912 → turns 17 in 1929)
    unemp_group = if_else(retail_q == 1, # categorical column based on unemployment tercile
                          "Lowest unemployment counties (Q1)",
                          "Highest unemployment counties (Q3)")
  )
ggplot(chart1_data, aes(x = xaxis, y = finish_share,
                        colour = unemp_group, linetype = unemp_group,
                        shape  = unemp_group)) +
  # Shade depression onset period
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = 1929.5, xmax = max(chart1_data$xaxis, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.5, # x bounds of box
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, # y bounds
           fill = "#DDDDDD", alpha = 0.35) + # colour of box
  annotate("text", x = 1930.2, y = Inf, # bounds
           label = "Depression\nonset →",
           vjust = 1.4, hjust = 0, size = 2.8, colour = "#666666", # colour and size
           family = "sans") + #font
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1929.5, linetype = "dashed", # line to signify depression onset
             color = "#888888", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) + # line chart
  geom_point(size = 2.2) + # with data points, on the line
  scale_color_manual(values = c( # colours of lines
    "Highest unemployment counties (Q3)" = economist_red,
    "Lowest unemployment counties (Q1)"  = "#555555")) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c( # type of lines in chart
    "Highest unemployment counties (Q3)" = "solid",
    "Lowest unemployment counties (Q1)"  = "solid"
  )) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c( # for points
    "Highest unemployment counties (Q3)" = 16,
    "Lowest unemployment counties (Q1)"  = 1
  )) +
  scale_x_continuous( # x-axis scale/values
    breaks = seq(1925, 1938, by = 1),
    labels = seq(1925, 1938, by = 1)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous( # y-axis scale/values
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0.38, 0.62)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Narrowing the Gap - Changing Opportunity Cost",
    subtitle = "Share completing high school (≥12 years), by county unemployment severity",
    x        = "Year cohort turns 17",
    y        = NULL,
    caption  = "Source: Janas (2025), Journal of Economic History. IPUMS linked census data.\nNote: x-axis shows the year each birth cohort reached age 17."
  ) +
  theme_custom() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Comparing cohorts born before and during the Great Depression, across different unemployment environments

chart2_data <- df %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(educ_cont), # keep rows where data for educ_cont is not missing
    !is.na(retail_q), # keep rows where retail_q is not missing
    cohort %in% c(1925, 1926, 1927, 1928, 1929, 1935, 1936, 1937) # keep data for cohort within values
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    period = if_else( # creates new column based on cohort year, if_else to categorical
      cohort <= 1929,
      "Pre-Depression (cohort 1925-29)",
      "Late Depression (cohort 1935-37)"
    ),
    period = factor(period, levels = c( # converts period to categorical variable 
      "Pre-Depression (cohort 1925-29)",
      "Late Depression (cohort 1935-37)"
    )),
    unemp_grp = case_when( # new categorical column based on unemployment values (terciles)
      retail_q == 1 ~ "Low unemployment (Q1)",
      retail_q == 2 ~ "Mid unemployment (Q2)",
      retail_q == 3 ~ "High unemployment (Q3)"
    ),
    unemp_grp = factor(unemp_grp, levels = c( # converts to categorical variables, in order
      "High unemployment (Q3)",
      "Mid unemployment (Q2)",
      "Low unemployment (Q1)"
    ))
  )

# message("Rows in chart2_data: ", nrow(chart2_data))
# count(chart2_data, period)
# Ridgeline plot comparing distribution of years of educations across unemployment terciles and cohorts 
  # use bins, instead of smoother, to prevent jagged lines
ggplot(chart2_data,
       aes(x      = educ_cont,
           y      = unemp_grp,
           fill   = unemp_grp,
           weight = probit_w)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    stat           = "binline", # bins, so not continuous (more like a histogram)
    bins           = 18,
    scale          = 0.9,
    draw_baseline  = FALSE,
    alpha          = 0.75,
    colour         = "white",
    linewidth      = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 12, linetype = "dashed", # vertical line to mark high school completion point
             colour = "black", linewidth = 0.45) +
  annotate("text", x = 12.4, y = 3.6, # text in ... position
           label = "High school\ncompletion",
           hjust = 0, size = 2.6,
           colour = "#555555", family = "sans") +
  scale_x_continuous(  # x-axis formatting
    breaks = c(0, 4, 8, 12, 16),
    limits = c(0, 18)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c( # colours for each ridge
    "High unemployment (Q3)" = economist_red,
    "Mid unemployment (Q2)"  = "#888888",
    "Low unemployment (Q1)"  = "#555555"
  )) +
  facet_wrap(~period, ncol = 2) + # two side by side plots for the different periods
  labs(
    title    = "Hard times, higher education",
    subtitle = "Distribution of years of schooling completed, by county unemployment\nseverity and cohort group. Dashed line marks high school completion.",
    x        = "Years of education completed",
    y        = NULL,
    caption  = "Source: Janas (2025), Journal of Economic History. Weighted by inverse probability weights."
  ) +
  theme_custom() +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "none",
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )

# clustering around 8-9 years, completion of middle/elementary
# low unemployment counties have little change during depression
# chart 1 quantifies shift, this chart shows underlying context (where about youth are completing school to, more granular)
# Interaction regression: finish_12 ~ unemployment x age_in_1930
# Separately for men (male == 1) and women (male == 0)
# Using feols() from fixest as the R equivalent of reghdfe

run_age_model <- function(gender_val, label) {
  d <- df %>%
    filter(
      male == gender_val, # selecting one gender
      !is.na(finish_12_rate), # where the following variables are not missing
      !is.na(delta_unemp_std),
      !is.na(age1930),
      is.finite(probit_w),
      age1930 %in% 11:23 # between specific ages
    )
  
   # Fixed effects ordinary least squares regression
      # Dependent: finish_12_rate
      # Independent: interaction of age1930 (factor) with delta_unemp_std, using age 18 as the reference category
        # i(age1930, delta_unemp_std, ref = "18") means model estimates how the effect of delta_unemp_std varies by age group, relative to age 18
      # Fixed effects: id_ (individual level) and cohort, controlling for unobserved heterogeneity
      # Weights: uses probit_w to weight observations
      # Clusted standard errors: clustered by id_ to account for within-individual correlation

  mod <- feols(
    finish_12_rate ~ i(age1930, delta_unemp_std, ref = 18) |
      id_ + cohort,
    data    = d,
    weights = ~probit_w,
    cluster = ~id_
  )

  # Extract coefficients safely using broom
  results <- tidy(mod, conf.int = TRUE, conf.level = 0.90) %>% # extract model coefficients with 90% confidence intervals
    filter(str_detect(term, "delta_unemp_std")) # filters results to keep only terms related to delta_unemp_std

  results %>%
    mutate(
      age    = as.integer(str_extract(term, "(?<=age1930::)\\d+")),
      gender = label
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(age))
}

chart3_data <- bind_rows(
  run_age_model(0, "Women"),
  run_age_model(1, "Men")
) %>%
  # Add reference age 18 as zero
  bind_rows(
    tibble(age = 18, estimate = 0, conf.low = 0, conf.high = 0,
           gender = c("Women", "Men"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(gender, age)
ggplot(chart3_data, aes(x = age, y = estimate,
                        color = gender, fill = gender, group = gender)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "#888888", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 13.5, xmax = 17.5,
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#FFDD99", alpha = 0.25) +
  annotate("text", x = 15.5, y = Inf,
           label = "Prime high-school years",
           vjust = 1.6, size = 2.7, colour = "#666600", family = "sans") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "Women" = economist_red,
    "Men" = economist_blue
  )) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Women" = economist_red,
    "Men" = economist_blue
    )) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(11, 23, by = 1),
    labels = seq(11, 23, by = 1)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Age during Depression determines education outcomes ",
    subtitle = "Effect on high school completion of a 1 standard deviation rise in local unemployment, \nby age in 1930 (reference: age 18). 90% confidence intervals shown.",
    x = "Age in 1930",
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Janas (2025), Journal of Economic History.\nEstimates from OLS with city and cohort fixed effects.\nNotes: Effect is relative to age-18 reference group. Shaded band highlights typical high-school enrolment ages."
  ) +
  theme_custom()
